---
title: "Research"
---

::: {.content-wrap .container-narrow}
My research centers on the durability, damage evolution, and predictive modeling of fiber-reinforced polymer composites in challenging environments. By combining mechanical testing, microstructural characterization, and finite element simulation, I aim to understand how environmental exposure alters material response and to develop reliable methods for performance prediction and structural design.

## Research Themes

### Environmental durability of CFRP composites
I investigate the long-term effects of seawater, hygrothermal exposure, hydrostatic pressure, and sustained mechanical loading on composite materials, with emphasis on strength degradation, moisture diffusion, and service reliability.

### Failure mechanisms and interfacial behavior
My work examines matrix cracking, interfacial debonding, delamination, fiber fracture, and failure-mode transition under complex environmental and loading conditions. A key goal is to clarify how microstructural damage evolves into macroscopic performance loss.

### Multiscale finite element modeling and prediction
I develop experimental–computational approaches for predicting degradation and failure in composite laminates. This includes moisture diffusion modeling, constitutive degradation, cohesive interface simulation, and data-informed strength prediction frameworks.

## Methods
My research combines:

- mechanical testing under marine and hygrothermal conditions;
- SEM, EDS, XRD, FTIR, and DMA characterization;
- Abaqus-based finite element simulation;
- Python-assisted data processing and model implementation;
- empirical and physics-informed prediction methods.

## Background
Yuchi Liu received his Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from Zhejiang University. From 2024 to 2025, he worked as a visiting researcher at the National University of Singapore. He is currently a postdoctoral researcher at Tsinghua University.
:::
